title: NOC 22313 – Aircraft Instrument, Electrical & Avionics Mechanics, Technicians and Inspectors
description: Aircraft avionics and electrical specialists are in strong demand across Canada’s aviation and transportation sectors—especially as airlines, MRO providers, and aircraft manufacturers expand fleets and modernize onboard systems. Classified under TEER 2, this occupation is well-suited for:
duties: Ensure compliance with safety and performance standards set by Transport Canada
employment_requirements: 2–3-year college diploma in avionics, electronics, or electrical technology
Four years apprenticeship or on-the-job training
For inspectors: 3–5 years of avionics experience
AME License (Category E – Avionics) issued by Transport Canada
Optional industry certification from Canadian Council for Aviation & Aerospace
In Quebec, regulatory-body membership is required to use the title Professional Technologist
job_outlook: This suggests continued hiring opportunities—especially for candidates with:
additional_information: Canadian aviation employers continue recruiting avionics and aircraft-electrical specialists for:
Fleet expansionDigital cockpit upgradesNavigation & communication systemsRegulatory-driven maintenance cycles
Typical annual earnings: $38,500–$92,200 CAD, depending on province, licensing, and specialization.

Aviation-specific PR eligibilityUnionized airline rolesExcellent safety-regulated work environmentsAccess to public healthcareLong-term career growth into inspection & certification rolesHigh demand in regional airports and MRO hubs
